headline: Trump officially nominates Kevin Warsh as Fed chair to replace Jerome Powell
summary: Sen. Thom Tillis has said he would block Kevin Warsh's nomination until a federal criminal investigation of Fed Chair Jerome Powell is dropped

headline: Tracking the gold trade: 3 reasons Wall Street is still bullish
summary: Gold (GC=F) prices have been volatile this week due to impacts from the Iran war, but overall, the precious metal has rallied over the past few years as investors continue to seek safety amid increased uncertainty. Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman tracks the precious metal's biggest milestones over the past several years, while Yahoo Finance Senior Business Reporter Ines Ferré takes a look at recent calls on gold from firms like JPMorgan (JPM), Goldman Sachs (GS), and Wells Fargo (WFC), explaining why Wall Street is still so bullish on the commodity. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: Coinbase leads crypto stocks higher after Trump signals support for digital asset market structure bill
summary: Coinbase jumped 12% on Wednesday following President Trump's latest call to help pass a law that would enable crypto firms to issue yield-bearing stablecoins.

headline: How The Church & Dwight (CHD) Investment Story Is Shifting With New 2026 Expectations
summary: Our updated fair value estimate for Church & Dwight has shifted from US$95.84 to US$103.58, reflecting an around 8% change in the modelled worth of the shares. This move sits alongside Street research where bullish targets cluster around US$110 to US$112 and more cautious views sit nearer US$97, all framed around how credible the 2026 outlook and sector setup look today. As you read on, you will see how these different price targets fit into a broader story you can track over time. Analyst...

headline: Trump sides with crypto firms in trillion-dollar battle with banks over stablecoin yield
summary: The dispute centers on whether crypto firms like Coinbase can offer yields on stablecoins, which banks warn will siphon trillions of dollars from the industry.
